title: Continuous Compliance Monitoring (CCM) Platform
summary: Event-driven platform that continuously verifies controls at 100% sample coverage across 10+ systems, sub-second at scale.
about: An enterprise **Continuous Compliance Monitoring** platform that replaces periodic, sample-based auditing with always-on, **100% sample** evaluation. It monitors changes, access, and configuration across **10+ integrated systems**, flags discrepancies in real time, and delivers an end-to-end solution from ingestion to alerting and reporting. Built on an **event-driven**, **publish-subscribe** architecture with horizontally scalable consumers, engineered for a sub-500ms p99 latency target.
impact: Sustains **10,000+ events/day** with sub-second processing under peak load and enables an estimated **5,000+ automated hours/year**, while moving compliance assurance from periodic sampling to continuous full coverage.
learning: Designing a low-latency (sub-500ms p99) event-driven control-monitoring system, scaling asynchronous pub-sub consumers horizontally, and delivering ingestion, evaluation, notification, and dashboarding as one end-to-end platform.

title: AI-Powered Dashboard Generator
summary: Generative-AI tool that builds any ServiceNow dashboard from a natural-language prompt.
about: A generative-AI tool that creates dashboards on the **ServiceNow** platform directly from a **natural-language prompt** - a user describes the dashboard they want and the system assembles it, supporting virtually any dashboard type. A **large language model** (accessed via **REST**) interprets the request and maps it onto the platform's reporting schema (tables, aggregations, and visualizations), which is then generated through the platform's dashboard APIs.
impact: Democratizes dashboard creation - non-technical stakeholders can stand up tailored dashboards by describing them, removing the dependency on specialists and the manual report-configuration effort dashboards normally require.
learning: Applying an LLM to translate natural language into a concrete platform data/visualization specification, and generating platform-native dashboards programmatically via APIs.

title: Access Review & Governance Platform
summary: Event-driven, microservices platform that automates access reviews and certification, with zero audit failures.
about: An **event-driven** access governance platform that automates how access rights are reviewed, certified, and audited across the enterprise. Built as **Node.js microservices** communicating through an internal **asynchronous event broker**, it processes access events (grants, revocations, role changes) in real time, evaluates them against governance policies, drives review/certification workflows, and persists a queryable audit trail.
impact: Delivered end to end; eliminated **800+ manual hours/year** of access-review effort and achieved **zero audit failures** across all compliance cycles in which it operated.
learning: Designing a decoupled, event-driven microservices system around an internal message broker, and turning a manual, finding-prone certification process into a reliable, continuously auditable one.

title: Eco Project
summary: REST API backend for an ecology blogging platform, built with Express and MongoDB.
about: The backend half of an ecology-themed blogging application, exposing a JSON REST API built on **Node.js** and **Express**. It persists blogs and users in **MongoDB Atlas** through **Mongoose** schemas, with `/blogs` supporting full CRUD and `/users` supporting list and add. CORS and dotenv handle cross-origin requests and environment-based configuration.
impact: A student/learning project (the backend repo of a two-part app) that demonstrates a working CRUD API for environmental blog content. Its value is primarily educational rather than production-grade.
learning: Practiced building a clean Express router/model structure with Mongoose schemas (including required, unique, and minlength validation), connecting to a cloud MongoDB Atlas cluster, and exposing CRUD endpoints over HTTP.

title: Bytify
summary: A Python/Tkinter MIPS assembly simulator with pipeline visualization and a two-level cache model.
about: **Bytify** is a desktop **MIPS assembly language simulator** written in **Python** with a **Tkinter** GUI. It parses assembly programs, executes the core MIPS instruction set, and visualizes a classic 5-stage instruction pipeline alongside a two-level (L1/L2) cache model. Users can load a program, edit instructions in place, step through execution, and watch registers, stalls, and data-forwarding update live.
impact: An educational project that makes abstract computer-architecture concepts tangible by letting students run real MIPS code and see pipeline stalls, data forwarding, average IPC, and cache hit rates in a GUI.
learning: Required implementing a full instruction parser/decoder/executor plus modeling pipeline hazards (data dependency, load-use, branch prediction) and an LRU write-through two-level cache from scratch, wired into a reactive Tkinter interface.

title: Histainability
summary: Android app linking Google N-gram history trends to the UN's 17 Sustainable Development Goals, with quizzes.
about: **Histainability** is a native **Android** app (in **Kotlin**) that explores the UN's 17 Sustainable Development Goals through the lens of history. For a chosen goal it queries the **Google Books N-gram** corpus over **OkHttp3**, parses the timeseries with **Gson**, and plots it as an interactive line chart via **MPAndroidChart** alongside curated historical event cards. A built-in quiz lets users test their knowledge, and the UI supports dark/light themes and English/Hindi/Telugu localization.
impact: A hackathon/student-scale educational prototype showing how public n-gram data can be turned into an engaging awareness tool around sustainability goals. Content is hardcoded for the 17 SDGs with no backend or persistence.
learning: Taught practical Android integration of a third-party charting library with live REST data, asynchronous networking with OkHttp callbacks, and accessibility touches like theming and multi-language support.

title: Meet your Dronacharya
summary: MERN career-guidance community platform with Q&A, mentor experiences, real-time chat, and a custom NLP chatbot.
about: **Meet your Dronacharya** is a MERN-stack (**React**, **Node/Express**, **MongoDB**) community platform that helps students discover and pursue a career path with help from peers and experts. Users can ask career doubts, read mentor experiences, share study material, take a career-fit quiz, and chat in real time via **Socket.io**. It adds a from-scratch stemming/tokenization chatbot and **Akismet**-based spam detection on posts.
impact: A substantial student team project (~320 commits over three months) deployed on Netlify, covering auth, Q&A, experiences, study material, timelines, dashboards, and chat.
learning: Building a full MERN app end to end taught real-time messaging with Socket.io, secure password handling with bcrypt and Nodemailer OTP recovery, third-party API integration (Akismet, Google OAuth), and basic NLP for a custom chatbot.

title: Helping Nemo
summary: 2D Unity awareness game where a robot fish cleans ocean pollution across five levels plus survival mode.
about: **Helping Nemo** is a 2D environmental-awareness game built in **Unity** with **C#**, where Nemo, a robot fish, cleans polluted oceans. Players collect garbage, rescue netted turtles, and suck up oil spills across five progressively harder levels and an endless survival mode. It uses Unity's 2D physics, TextMeshPro UI, and Timeline cutscenes, and is deployed as **WebGL** to run in any modern browser.
impact: A five-person student team project hosted publicly on simmer.io to spread awareness about ocean pollution. Scope is a complete, playable browser game rather than a commercial product.
learning: Taught core Unity 2D game development including player controllers, spawner systems, score/progress UI, level transitions, and packaging a game for WebGL deployment.

title: UwU Compiler
summary: A full compiler for a custom language that lexes, parses, and generates MIPS assembly runnable on QtSpim.
about: A complete teaching-style compiler for a bespoke programming language called **UwU**, implemented across the classic compilation stages. The lexer is written in **Lex/Flex** and the parser/semantic analyzer in **Yacc/Bison** with an LALR(1) grammar; three-address intermediate code is emitted by **C++** semantic actions, and a **Python** backend lowers that IR into **MIPS assembly** runnable on QtSpim.
impact: A complete academic compiler-construction project demonstrating an end-to-end pipeline from source text to executable MIPS, including a symbol table, parse tree, and register allocation. Scope is a teaching language targeting simulation in QtSpim.
learning: Built hands-on understanding of every compiler phase, the practical use of Lex/Yacc tooling, S-attributed grammars, three-address IR design, and register allocation, while decoupling code generation so the target could be swapped (x86 to MIPS).

title: CodeVision
summary: VS Code accessibility extension that turns project and Python code structure into a navigable, spoken tree.
about: CodeVision (the "Blind Debugger Extension") is a **VS Code extension** written in **TypeScript** that acts as an eye for visually impaired programmers. It scans the open workspace into a folder/file tree and parses **Python** files into class/function/import nodes, which users navigate via keyboard bindings or **Webview UI Toolkit** buttons. Each node is read aloud using a text-to-speech library, with errors spoken in a distinct voice.
impact: A student/hackathon-style accessibility prototype demonstrating an audio-first code-navigation workflow; functional but static and Windows-leaning, so a proof of concept rather than a production tool.
learning: Required learning the VS Code extension lifecycle and webview message-passing model, integrating native text-to-speech, and writing a custom indentation-based parser to convert OS tree output and Python source into a traversable n-ary tree.

title: KBC Tirutsava
summary: A web-based "Kaun Banega Crorepati" quiz game built to run the KBC event at the Tirutsava college fest.
about: A **React** single-page app that recreates the TV game show "Kaun Banega Crorepati" for the Tirutsava college fest. A quiz master runs the game from a browser, pulling questions from **MongoDB** via an **Express/Mongoose** REST backend, with a 13-level prize ladder, configurable checkpoints, per-level timers, and classic lifelines (50/50, Expert Advice, Flip the Question, Phone a Friend).
impact: An event-utility project intended for in-person use at the fest, where one operator drives the quiz on a projector while a participant plays. A functional prototype scoped to a single live event.
learning: Taught full-stack CRUD with a React frontend talking to an Express/Mongoose API over Axios, plus managing complex game state (timers, lifelines, prize ladder, win/lose conditions) with React hooks.

title: RL-Based HPC Scheduler with Runtime Estimation
summary: B.Tech thesis: an RL HPC job scheduler that predicts job runtimes via code embeddings to improve Deep Q-Learning scheduling.
about: A B.Tech final-year thesis (IIT Tirupati) that builds a **Reinforcement Learning** scheduler for **High-Performance Computing** clusters. Its key idea: replace the unreliable user-supplied job runtime in the scheduler's state with an ML-predicted runtime. Python job code is tokenized and embedded (**NLP**), hashed to an integer, and fed with CPU/memory requirements into a **Gradient Boosting** regressor (~90% accuracy); a **Deep Q-Learning** agent (built on DRAS) then schedules using those accurate runtimes.
impact: Feeding ML-approximated runtimes into the same RL scheduler improved average wait time by **42.35%**, max runtime (a job-starvation proxy) by **53.66%**, and system utilization by **9.2%** over the user-estimated baseline, and beat classical FCFS across all three metrics. An academic capstone validated in simulation (CQSim).
learning: Deep Q-Learning and reward-function design, code embeddings via NLP tokenization (user2code2vec-style), Gradient Boosting Regression with grid-searched hyperparameters, and HPC scheduling concepts like backfilling, starvation, and the CQSim simulator.

title: Personal Portfolio
summary: Full-stack portfolio with an admin CMS, a RAG AI chatbot, and an AI resume generator across three microservices.
about: A full-stack personal portfolio built as a three-service monorepo: a **React + Vite + TypeScript** SPA (Tailwind, React Query, Framer Motion), a **Node + Express + Mongoose** API, and a **Python + FastAPI** AI service running a **LangGraph** agent. It pairs a public site with a Google-OAuth admin CMS for projects, blogs, profile, skills and resumes. A **RAG chatbot** and an AI resume generator are powered by **Google Gemini**, with retrieval grounded in **MongoDB Atlas Vector Search**.
impact: A personal project demonstrating end-to-end ownership of a polyglot microservice architecture - frontend, REST backend, and a Python AI service - wired for free-tier deployment (Vercel + Render + Atlas).
learning: Designing a single-origin URL strategy (dev proxy / nginx / Vercel rewrite) that keeps cookies same-site, and building LLM resilience via a multi-model Gemini fallback chain plus a fixed embedding model to avoid corrupting the vector space.
